decks.notes: This deck focuses on utilizing powerful creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Territorial Kavu to aggressively attack the opponent. Leyline of the Guildpact and Leyline Binding help to control the opponent's board while Stubborn Denial offers protection for your creatures. Quantum Riddler and Scion of Draco provide additional value and card advantage. The mana base is diverse, allowing for consistent casting of spells and creatures. Overall, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with fast aggro plays and efficient control elements to secure victory in Modern 1 vs 1 matches.

The main focus of this Dimir Control deck is to disrupt the opponent's game plan by using a combination of counter spells, hand disruption, and removal spells. By controlling the board and limiting the opponent's resources, this deck aims to outvalue and outlast the opponent until it can establish a dominant position and win the game with powerful threats like Murktide Regent and Kaito, Bane of Nightmares. The deck also utilizes card draw and selection to consistently find the right answers at the right time.

This deck focuses on playing aggressive creatures and using efficient removal spells to control the board. The key strategy is to quickly establish a strong board presence with cards like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Scion of Draco while disrupting the opponent's game plan with cards like Fatal Push and Leyline Binding. The deck aims to pressure the opponent early and maintain momentum throughout the game, utilizing cards like Quantum Riddler and Leyline of the Guildpact to provide additional value. The versatility of the deck allows for adaptability to various matchups and strategies.

The main concept of this control deck is to utilize a combination of counterspells, removal spells, and card draw to control the board and slow down the opponent's game plan. By maintaining a high level of interaction and disruption, the deck aims to outvalue the opponent and establish dominance in the late game. Key cards like Teferi, Time Raveler and Teferi, Hero of Dominaria provide additional control tools and card advantage, while cards like Prismatic Ending and Solitude offer removal options for different types of threats. Overall, the goal is to outlast and outplay the opponent with careful decision making and resource management.

The deck focuses on assembling pieces to generate mana quickly and cast a high number of spells in a single turn, with the goal of casting multiple copies of Grapeshot to deal lethal damage to the opponent. It includes cards that help filter through the deck, generate mana, and recur spells from the graveyard to continue the combo. Additionally, the deck includes cards with alternate win conditions to provide versatility in different matchups. The key strategy is to chain together spells efficiently to overwhelm the opponent with a large storm count and finish the game with a powerful Grapeshot.

The deck focuses on assembling the UrzaTron lands (Urza's Tower, Power Plant, and Mine) to generate a large amount of colorless mana. By ramping up quickly, the deck aims to play powerful threats such as Ugin, Eye of the Storms, Emrakul, the Promised End, and Karn, the Great Creator. Additionally, the deck includes disruptive elements like Thought-Knot Seer and Chalice of the Void to control the opponent's resources. With a mix of big threats, mana acceleration, and disruption, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent and take control of the game.

This deck focuses on quickly assembling Tron lands (Urza's Tower, Mine, Power Plant) to generate a large amount of mana. It uses powerful Eldrazi creatures like Emrakul and Thought-Knot Seer to control the board and disrupt the opponent's strategy. Karn, the Great Creator and Ugin, the Eye of Storms provide additional control and threats. The deck also includes cards like Chalice of the Void and Trinisphere to slow down the opponent's plays. Overall, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with powerful threats and disruption while utilizing the massive mana advantage from Tron lands.

The deck focuses on using a variety of counter spells and removals to control the game, while also utilizing card draw and manipulation to maintain advantage. The deck features powerful planeswalkers like Teferi, Time Raveler and Jace, the Mind Sculptor to further control the board and disrupt the opponent's game plan. With a mix of creature removal, counterspells, and card advantage, the deck aims to outvalue and outmaneuver the opponent until it can secure the win with its powerful finishers.

Utilize Song of Creation to generate card advantage and cast multiple spells in a single turn, enabling explosive combo potential with cards like Emry, Lurker of the Loch and Mox Opal. The deck aims to quickly assemble its pieces and win the game through a combination of card draw, mana acceleration, and interaction to disrupt the opponent's game plan. It requires careful sequencing and resource management to maximize the effectiveness of its combo pieces and overwhelm the opponent with a flurry of spells and threats.

This deck focuses on quickly reanimating powerful creatures like Atraxa, Grand Unifier and Griselbrand using cards like Goryo's Vengeance and Fallaji Archaeologist. By utilizing instant spells such as Ephemerate and Solitude to protect our creatures and disrupt the opponent's game plan, we aim to create explosive turns that overwhelm our opponent. The deck also includes hand disruption with cards like Thoughtseize to control the pace of the game. Overall, the strategy revolves around comboing off with instant-speed reanimation and protecting our key creatures to dominate the battlefield.

This deck focuses on assembling a variety of creatures that can be searched for and brought onto the battlefield using cards like Green Sun's Zenith and Spymaster's Vault. Yawgmoth, Thran Physician is a key card that can be used to sacrifice creatures and generate value, while cards like Malevolent Rumble and Walking Ballista provide removal and flexibility. The deck aims to create a toolbox of creatures that can disrupt the opponent's strategy and generate advantage over time, ultimately leading to a win through a combination of creature synergies and control elements.

This deck focuses on aggressive creatures that can be repeatedly blinked, or temporarily removed from the battlefield and brought back. By utilizing cards like Ephemerate and Flickerwisp, the deck aims to maintain board presence and disrupt the opponent's strategy. A combination of creatures with powerful abilities and efficient removal spells help to control the game and push through damage quickly. The deck also includes some hand disruption with cards like Thoughtseize to further disrupt the opponent's plans. Overall, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of aggression and disruption.

This deck focuses on using instant reanimation spells like Goryo's Vengeance to quickly bring back powerful creatures like Griselbrand or Atraxa, Grand Unifier from the graveyard. The deck also utilizes cards like Psychic Frog and Fallaji Archaeologist to control the board and disrupt the opponent's strategy. Additionally, cards like Ephemerate and Otherworldly Gaze help to dig for combo pieces and protect key creatures. Overall, the deck aims to combo off and overwhelm the opponent with powerful threats while maintaining control of the game.

The deck focuses on generating mana quickly through rituals and medallions to cast powerful spells that deal damage, while also using card draw and recursion to maintain momentum. The goal is to assemble the pieces needed for the combo, such as Ral, Monsoon Mage and Grapeshot, to finish off the opponent in a single turn. The deck utilizes a mix of efficient spells and lands to create explosive turns and overwhelm the opponent with a flurry of spells. This deck is built to quickly execute its combo and win the game before the opponent can mount a defense.

The deck focuses on using aggressive creatures and spells to quickly overwhelm the opponent while taking advantage of flicker effects to generate additional value from enters-the-battlefield effects. By utilizing a combination of aggressive creatures like Solitude and Emperor of Bones with powerful flicker effects like Ephemerate and Flickerwisp, the deck aims to outpace the opponent and control the battlefield. Additionally, disruptive spells like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push help to disrupt the opponent's game plan while powerful finishers like Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd and Overlord of the Balemurk help to close out the game.

The main focus of this deck is to generate a lot of mana quickly using cards like Eldrazi Temple and Urza's Saga, while also utilizing cards like Grove of the Burnwillows and Malevolent Rumble to control the board. The deck aims to create a powerful board presence with creatures like Basking Broodscale and Emrakul, the Promised End, while also setting up combos with cards like Sowing Mycospawn and Blade of the Bloodchief. Ultimately, the goal is to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of fast mana generation, board control, and powerful creature synergies.
